claims.claim: Moderate egg consumption (up to one egg per day) is not associated with cardiovascular disease risk overall.
Moderate egg consumption (up to one egg per day) is associated with potentially lower cardiovascular disease risk in Asian populations.
In the pooled multivariable analysis, consumption of at least one egg per day was not associated with incident cardiovascular disease risk after adjustment for updated lifestyle and dietary factors.
The study population included participants from three large US cohorts (NHS, NHS II, and HPFS) who were free of cardiovascular disease, type 2 diabetes, and cancer at baseline.
